It doesn't help that people conflate AI generated "art", and AI gen being used for research references or materials, a bit like speed tuning a Google search in essence

Not that the latter is great either...but it's completely different from AI-generated art, even BIAV said they tried out that method at some point

Again, it's not great, but those are very different things, and due to the AI crap and panic going on right now, people conflate the two and accuse anything and everything of being AI generated due to paranoia

For example, the latter case (Artists who would use it as a tool for speed tuning research refs/materials) is what's being experimented with one of the hoyo game right now (nexus anima) , and what has been tried by BIAV at some point, but the art itself (whether finalized, concept art, drawn research etc.) is ***not*** AI-generated whatsoever 

The former is what's starting to be rampant in very cheap games with an easy cash grab goal in mind
new technology and luddism goes hand in hand
people used to be against power lines for Chris's sake, but meh, they are fighting a losing battle, nothing that we should be concerned about.
The issue is introducing proper guidelines and rules most of the time, especially in a proper work environment
Like, there are ways to use those thing as ***tools***, but they were pretty much dropped in the wild all of sudden
tools vs non tools is not a legal distinction, such guidlines can't exist

There is a difference in the way you use it at least, if there can't be a guidelines regarding the definition, there can be some regarding limitations and specificities of for which action or step you can use it

At least that's how it works in my field (art), such things are starting to be more common
Like the use I described earlier
right, it's mostly a self imposed guideline, not an industry standard
people would still be paranoid all the time, you can't help it
At the start it's often like that yes, and oftentimes some sort of industry standard starts appearing, but it takes time
And the fact that it takes time is part of the issue
Coupled with internet being what it is today

and the fact that people involved in industry actually agree with you, there's a huge incentive for AI assist and AI generated art, since at the end of the day it's just a product like the cloth we wear
You can't predict whether those standards will spread in the span of 1 or 10 years sadly

Actual "AI generated art" no.

But "AI assist" yes, especially in early stages of research, but again, no actual AI is involved in anything that has to do with drawing.
The distinction is very important

Right now people are trying to figure out the most productive way to use it in those specific stages, while ***still*** not losing creativity in those initial stages (that are akin to streamlining "google refs", using the old way of speech, so nothing to do with the actual execution of concept art of the act of drawing in general)
yeah, it just so there's industry people who think AI has a role to play in drawing while still being creative
you can foster a reputation for your brand, but the industry as whole don't have the same opinion on this

Yeah, although execution itself is meant to be fully human made 

But there is a way to use those things as tools

Unfortunately meanwhile some companies fire people for nothing because they don't know how to let actual artists use them as tools, and think they can "replace" them

What a travesty
And well, sadly art isn't the only field concerned by this, it will take time for things to stabilize
But people can never get that time back
textile machines are just tools too, you still need operators and workers for them
it's just replacement can't be avoided when now less people can do more work
of course there's always going to be an artist in the loop somewhere

You can't exactly compare those two things, drawing is a different process 

In the textile you don't create the thread, you ***use*** it 

In drawing, aside of the pencils and everything, you create everything from scratch, with techniques and human "variations" (that are part of the core of drawing) that simply can't be properly recreated
Anyway I'm done with this discussion, I said what I had to say as something working in the field
